latest.title: teide volcano ash in sky
latest.script_key: teide s ash isn t ash that black cloud it s pulverized rock volcanoes don t just burn things they grind mountains into dust that can circle the planet watch that column climb millions of tons of rock turned to powder in minutes that s why planes reroute no fireworks just a slow motion earthquake in the sky

latest.title: volcano crater from above spewing lava
latest.script_key: watch this volcano s throat pulse that dark hole isn t empty it s where the earth exhales hot rock bursts up like blood in a stethoscope but this heartbeat is building mountains in real time that s why the crater rim glows red ever seen a planet rewriting itself from the sky

latest.title: mountains don't erode how you think
latest.script_key: mountains don t erode like you think those jagged peaks the rocks aren t just crumbling the real action happens inside the mountain ice splits rock water seeps in and the whole thing just slowly unfolds that s why the trail keeps getting steeper ever notice how the rocks look half melted that s erosion in progress

latest.title: earth’s storms glow like neon veins from space
latest.script_key: watch earth s storms light up at night those glowing veins aren t city lights they re lightning flickering through storms we never see from down here that s why satellites catch it but we miss the show here s the part nobody tells you every flash is a tiny war between air and water happening right now above your head

latest.title: Conservation helps forests recover damaged ground

latest.title: Storm clouds reveal a storm's heat engine

latest.title: Cells copy instructions before splitting
latest.script_key: cells have to copy themselves before dividing watch the cell boundary because division only works after the internal instructions are copied and organized then the cell can split the material into two working lives at this scale reproduction is careful logistics which tiny process next
